sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me carefully map the button layout from the initial state (history[0].frame)
# and understand the column alignment with the checkerboard

init_frame = history[0].frame
init_ascii = init_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Full button row
print("Initial row 42 (cols 14-50):")
print(f"  {init_ascii[42][14:50]}")
print(f"  {''.join(str(i%10) for i in range(14, 50))}")

# Let me identify all button groups
# Horizontal buttons at row 42
# Vertical buttons at rows 44-46
# Let me find contiguous non-W segments at row 42

row42 = init_ascii[42]
i = 14
while i < 50:
    if row42[i] != 'W':
        start = i
        while i < 50 and row42[i] != 'W':
            i += 1
        print(f"  Horizontal button: cols {start}-{i-1}, chars: {row42[start:i]}")
    else:
        i += 1

# Vertical buttons
print("\nVertical buttons (rows 44-46):")
for col in range(14, 50):
    chars = init_ascii[44][col] + init_ascii[45][col] + init_ascii[46][col]
    if chars != 'WWW':
        print(f"  Col {col}: {chars}")

# Now let me look at the checkerboard column alignment
# The checkerboard is cols 14-49, with 4x4 squares
# Each square is 4x4, so columns are: 14-17, 18-21, 22-25, 26-29, 30-33, 34-37, 42-45, 46-49
print("\nCheckerboard column groups:")
for start in range(14, 50, 4):
    end = start + 3
    print(f"  Cols {start}-{end}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
